美国联邦调查局逮捕了一名俄罗斯公民，原因是他试图招募一名员工在内华达州的一家公司内植入恶意软件。
据美国司法部称，据称，27岁的埃戈尔·伊戈雷维奇·克里奇科夫(Egor Igorevich Kriuchkov)承诺向这名员工支付高达100万美元。
从8月3日开始，Kriuchkov一直在与这名员工合谋植入恶意软件。其目标是：从这家未具名的内华达州公司窃取数据，然后威胁要将信息公之于众，除非支付巨额赎金。
该计划可望从受害人公司取得四百万元。然而，司法部的起诉书表明，这名员工最终与联邦调查局秘密合作，收集对Kriuchkov不利的证据，Kriuchkov后来于周六在洛杉矶被捕。
据称，这起阴谋始于7月16日，当时Kriuchkov通过双方熟人通过WhatsApp联系了这名员工。Kriuchkov随后从俄罗斯飞往美国，与这名员工一起去太浩湖旅行。8月3日，这位俄罗斯国民透露了他会面的真正原因，称他为专门劝说公司的“团体”工作。
“Kriuchkov继续解释说，‘集团’付钱给目标公司的员工，让他们将恶意软件引入目标公司的计算机系统，”FBI的起诉书说。“Kriuchkov说，‘集团’已经在多个场合成功地执行了这些‘特别项目’，并确定了一些目标公司。”
至于恶意软件，Kriuchkov告诉这名员工，它的设计初衷是首先通过受害者公司网络中类似DDOS的中断来创造一个诡计。然而，真正的主要攻击将从该公司的数据库中掠夺数据，并将其发送回Kriuchkov工作的组织。
起初，Kriuchkov打算通过比特币或现金向员工支付50万美元。但在员工要求更高后，他同意支付100万美元。
FBI在起诉书中写道：“为了缓解(受害员工)对被抓的担忧，Kriuchkov声称‘集团’参与的最古老的‘项目’发生在三年半前，而‘集团’的增选成员仍然为公司工作。”
克里奇科夫是否与俄罗斯政府有任何联系尚未透露。但根据起诉书，他与一名黑客共事，这名黑客也是“俄罗斯一家政府银行的高级雇员”。
Kriuchkov可能在本月他的神秘组织决定推迟该项目之前，正在努力在内华达州公司内部植入恶意软件。在此期间，联邦调查局设法收集了对这名俄罗斯国民不利的证据，部分原因是监视了他与这名员工的会面。
负责拉斯维加斯分部的联邦调查局特工亚伦·罗斯(Aaron Rouse)在今天的声明中表示：“在这件事上，联邦调查局再次能够在任何损害发生之前进行干预。”
周一，克里奇科夫首次出现在联邦法院。他现在面临法定的最高刑期为5年监禁和25万美元的罚款。

     The FBI has arrested a Russian citizen for trying to recruit an employee to plant malware inside a Nevada-based company.
 Allegedly, the 27-year-old Egor Igorevich Kriuchkov was promising to pay as much as $1 million to the employee,  according to the US Justice Department.
 Starting on August 3rd, Kriuchkov had been conspiring with the employee to plant the malware. The goal: to steal data from the unnamed Nevada-based company and then threaten to make the information public, unless a large ransom was paid.
 The scheme was expected to fetch $4 million from the victim company. However, the Justice Department’s  complaint suggests the employee ended up secretly working with the FBI to gather evidence against Kriuchkov, who was later arrested on Saturday in Los Angeles.
 The conspiracy allegedly started on July 16th when Kriuchkov contacted the employee over WhatsApp through a mutual acquaintance. Kriuchkov then flew from Russia to the US to travel with the employee for a trip to Lake Tahoe. On August 3rd, the Russian national then revealed his true reason for meeting up, saying he worked for “group” that specializes in exhorting companies.
 “Kriuchkov went on to explain that the “group” pays employees of target companies to introduce malware into the target company’s computer system,” the FBI’s complaint says. “Kriuchkov said the ‘group’ has performed these ‘special projects’ successfully on multiple occasions, and identified some of the targeted companies.”
 As for the malware, Kriuchkov told the employee it was designed to first create a ruse through a  DDOS-like disruption in the victim’s corporate network. However, the real primary attack will loot data from the company's databases and send it back to the group Kriuchkov was working for.
 Initially, Kriuchkov was going to pay the employee $500,000 through Bitcoin or cash. But then he agreed to pay $1 million after the employee demanded more.
 “To ease [the victim employees’] concerns about getting caught, Kriuchkov claimed the oldest ‘project’ the ‘group’ had worked on took place three and a half years ago and the ‘group’s’ co-optee still worked for the company,” the FBI’s complaint says.
  Whether Kriuchkov has any ties to the Russian government was left unsaid. But according to the complaint, he worked with a hacker who was also a “high level employee of a government bank in Russia.”
 Kriuchkov was working to plant the malware inside the Nevada company possibly this month before his shadowy group decide to delay the project. During that time, the FBI managed to gather evidence against the Russian national partly by surveilling his meetings with the employee.
 “In this matter, the FBI was once again able to intervene before any damage could occur,” said Aaron Rouse, FBI special agent in charge of the Las Vegas division, in today’s announcement.
 On Monday, Kriuchkov made his first appearance before a federal court. He now faces a statutory maximum sentence of five years in prison and a $250,000 fine.
title: 俄罗斯人被控试图招募员工在美国公司植入恶意软件
title_en: Russian Charged with Trying to Recruit Employee to Plant Malware in US Company

在世纪之交摧毁音乐行业的文件共享公司Napster以7000万美元的价格被出售给英国科技公司MelodyVR。
此次从美国公司RealNetworks收购是这个著名的科技繁荣品牌转型的最新尝试，该品牌由数字先驱肖恩·帕克(Sean Parker)于1999年共同创立，他后来成为Facebook的早期投资者。
Napster作为一个允许数百万人在互联网上非法分享音乐的网站而成名，在作为一个流媒体平台复活之前，它在2001年被关闭。
MelodyVR与Live Nation等推广者合作，以流媒体形式播放虚拟音乐、音乐会和活动。该公司首席执行官安东尼·马切特(Anthony Matchett)向英国“金融时报”表示，他有信心通过将其羽翼未丰的虚拟现场音乐业务与Napster的流媒体业务相结合，在音乐领域打造一家强大的公司。
他表示：“此次收购使我们作为一个集团很快走上了盈利之路。”
收购价格包括1500万美元的现金，1100万美元的MelodyVR股票，以及4400万美元对各种音乐出版商和唱片公司的付款义务。对于这家亏损的英国初创企业来说，这笔历史性的债务看起来是一个沉重的负担，尽管马切特表示，这些债务是“可控的”。
该公司2019年的收入不到20万GB，税前亏损1610万GB，因此Napster的交易代表着对该业务的反向收购。
在其全盛时期，Napster激起了音乐界的愤怒，重金属乐队Metallica成功起诉了该公司。该网站的关闭被证明是该行业的一个转折点，它的成功引发了CD销量的大幅下降，并转向通过苹果的iTunes下载音乐，后来又通过Spotify等法律服务进行流媒体播放。
Napster作为下载服务重新推出，与iTunes竞争，后来转变为一个流媒体平台，既以自己的品牌运营，又作为其他公司的服务提供商。它已经建立了一个拥有300万用户的基础和一个拥有9000万首音乐的歌曲库。
RealNetworks去年控制了Napster背后的母公司Rhapsody。Napster之前的投资者包括电子产品零售商百思买(Best Buy)和西班牙电信公司Telefónica。
马切特表示，尽管Spotify和苹果的规模巨大，但他仍然相信流媒体市场上有容纳规模较小的公司的空间。

note_en:
Napster, the file-sharing business that devastated the music industry at the turn of the century, has been sold to UK technology company MelodyVR in a $70 million deal.
 The acquisition from US company RealNetworks is the latest attempt to transform the famous tech boom brand, which was cofounded in 1999 by digital pioneer Sean Parker, who went on to be an early Facebook investor.
 Having found fame as a website that allowed millions of people to share music illegally over the internet, Napster was shut down in 2001 before being resurrected as a streaming platform.
 MelodyVR streams virtual music concerts and events in conjunction with promoters including Live Nation. Chief executive Anthony Matchett told the Financial Times he was confident he could create a strong player in the music sector by combining its fledgling virtual live music business with Napster’s streaming operation.
 “This acquisition puts us on a path to being profitable as a group pretty quickly,” he said.
 The purchase price comprises $15 million in cash, $11 million in MelodyVR shares and $44 million in payment obligations to various music publishers and record labels. That historic debt looks to be a heavy burden for the loss-making UK start-up, though Mr Matchett said the obligations are “manageable.”
 The company reported less than £200,000 in revenue in 2019 when it recorded a £16.1 million pre-tax loss, so the Napster deal represents a reverse takeover of the business.
 During its heyday, Napster raised the ire of the music industry, with the heavy metal band Metallica successfully suing the company. The site’s closure proved to be a turning point for the sector, with its success triggering a sharp decline in CD sales and a shift towards downloading music via Apple’s iTunes, and later streaming through legal services such as Spotify.
 Napster was relaunched as a download service to take on iTunes and later converted to a streaming platform that operates both under its own brand and as a service provider to other companies. It has built a base of 3m users and a song library of 90m pieces of music.
 RealNetworks took control of Rhapsody, the parent company behind Napster, last year. Previous Napster investors include Best Buy, the electronics retailer, and Spanish telecoms company Telefónica.
 Mr Matchett said he remained confident there was room for smaller players in the streaming market despite the huge scale of Spotify and Apple.

title: Napster以一首歌的价格卖给了英国虚拟活动公司
title_en: Napster sold for a song to UK virtual events company

总部位于加利福尼亚州圣莫尼卡的票务和优惠购买应用Atom Tickets终于在Snapchat内推出了其品牌购票体验。
Atom Tickets原计划在夏季早些时候推出，但由于流行病导致全国各地电影院关闭，其计划被搁置。
随着影院重新开放，Tenet、Bill和Ted等新片面临后果，神奇女侠和新变种人都出现在银幕上，似乎是时候推销这项新服务了。
Atom Tickets的联合创始人兼董事长马修·巴卡尔(Matthew Bakal)在一份声明中表示：“由于安全措施已经到位，影迷们急于重返电影，我们知道在Snapchat上推出Atom的票务体验是正确的时机。”“我们很高兴能提供一种安全、非接触式的数字服务，为影迷提供他们想要的东西--有一点时间逃离，欣赏一场电影。我们相信，Atom Mini的新电影票将建立在我们现有的DNA基础上，作为一个社交驱动的平台，它将使看电影变得更容易，并将朋友们聚集在一起。“。
Snap Mini是Snapchat中提供的服务，可以通过公司的消息传递界面访问。有了Atom的电影票，用户可以购买门票，以直接消息、群聊或故事的形式分享帖子的细节，并包括一个链接，让朋友们可以找到附近的座位。
“能够在Snapchat上推出Atom的电影票，我们非常兴奋。Atom已经为Snap Minis平台建立了无缝的电子商务体验，为北美各地的Snapchat用户带来了最好的Atom门票。“Snap，Inc.的平台伙伴关系总监Alston Cheek在一份声明中说。
Atom与许多新开业的电影院建立了合作关系，其中包括：AMC剧院、Cinemark、Harkins Theatres、National Amusements‘Showcase Cinemas、CMX Cinemas、Landmark in Cinemas、Studio Movie Grill、Malco Theatres、Landmark Theatres、ArcLight Cinemas和Larry H.Miller Megaplex Theatres等。
note_en:
Santa Monica, Calif.-based  Atom Tickets, the ticketing and concessions buying app, is finally launching its branded ticket buying experience inside Snapchat.
  Atom Tickets had planned to launch earlier in the summer, but its plans were put on hold by the pandemic-induced shutdown of movie theaters around the country.
  With theaters reopening and new titles like Tenet, Bill and Ted Face the Music, Wonder Woman, and The New Mutants all coming to the silver screen, the time seemed right to pitch the new service.
 “With safety measures in place and movie fans eager to return to the movies, we knew the timing was right to launch Atom’s ticketing experience on Snapchat,” said Matthew Bakal, Co-Founder and Chairman of Atom Tickets, in a statement. “We’re happy to provide a safe, contactless digital service that gives movie fans what they want – a little time to escape and enjoy a movie. We’re sure the new Movie Tickets by Atom Mini, which builds upon our existing DNA as a socially driven platform, will make moviegoing easier and bring friends together.”
 Snap Minis are services that are offered within Snapchat and can be accessed through the company’s messaging interface. With Movie Tickets by Atom, users can buy a ticket, share a post with the details in a direct message, group chat or story and include a link to allow friends to find seats nearby.
 “We couldn’t be more excited to have Movie Tickets by Atom launch on Snapchat. Atom has built a seamless ecommerce experience for the Snap Minis platform, bringing the best of Atom Tickets to Snapchatters across North America,” said Alston Cheek, Director of Platform Partnerships at Snap, Inc., in a statement.
 Atom has relationships with a slew of newly reopening movie theaters including: AMC Theatres, Cinemark, Harkins Theatres, National Amusements’ Showcase Cinemas, CMX Cinemas, Landmark in Cinemas of Canada, Studio Movie Grill, Malco Theatres, Landmark Theatres, ArcLight Cinemas, and Larry H. Miller Megaplex Theatres, among others.
title: 随着影院重新开张，Atom Tickets推出了Snap迷你应用程序
title_en: With theaters opening back up, Atom Tickets launches its Snap mini app

在竞争对手继续大量生产旗舰和中端设备之际，华硕对其新款手机一直采取谨慎的态度。以ROG Phone 3为例：它基本上只是一款升级版的ROG Phone II。今天发布的ZenFone 7系列也是如此，这两款手机都(基本上)是ZenFone 6的5G放大版。
显然，人们真的很喜欢ZenFone6上的翻转摄像头，以至于华硕决定在2020年的旗舰产品中保留这一功能。这意味着你可以使用主相机来拍摄更好的自拍，而不是凑合着用较弱的打孔式相机。这款新的翻盖相机配备了三个摄像头，而不是两个。
ZenFone7系列由两款机型组成：ZenFone7 Pro和几乎相同的ZenFone7。我在这里将重点放在Pro上，因为那是我一直在玩的一款。
note_en:
ASUS has been taking a cautious approach to its new phones, at a time when rivals continue to churn out a mix of flagship and midrange devices. Take the  ROG Phone 3: it’s basically just an updated ROG Phone II. The same goes for the ZenFone 7 series announced today, with both models being (pretty much) scaled-up, 5G-capable versions of the  ZenFone 6.
 Apparently people really liked the flip camera on the ZenFone 6, so much that ASUS decided to keep this feature for its 2020 flagship. It means you can use the main cameras for better selfies, instead of making do with a weaker punch-hole camera. This new flip camera comes with three cameras instead of two.
  The ZenFone 7 series consists of two models: the ZenFone 7 Pro and the near-identical ZenFone 7. I’ll be focusing on the Pro here, since that’s the one I’ve been playing with.
title: 实际操作华硕ZenFone 7 Pro，售价约950美元，配备可翻转摄像头、骁龙865 Plus、6.67英寸90 Hz AMOLED显示屏和5000毫安电池

埃隆·马斯克曾表示，他的神秘神经科技公司Neuralink将在周五美国东部时间下午6点展示一款工作正常的“设备”，大概是一个脑机接口。马斯克曾多次表示，他相信BMI设备是通过补充我们的脑力来帮助人类跟上人工智能的需要，但现在，他的目标要简单得多：创造一种可以让人们用大脑控制手机或电脑的植入式设备。
马斯克最初在7月份宣布了8月28日的“最新进展”，现在提供了更多将展示的细节。他说，更新将包括揭开第二代机器人的面纱，该机器人旨在将公司的技术连接到大脑上，并演示神经元“实时放电”，尽管目前还不清楚这到底是什么意思。
即使与马斯克的其他合资企业如特斯拉和SpaceX相比，Neuralink也是雄心勃勃的。该公司希望使用比人类头发还细的柔性电极来连接大脑，这种电极被称为“线”。目前的BMI设备在这项工作中使用僵硬的电极，这可能会造成损害。但插入柔性电极是一项更微妙、更具挑战性的任务，因此该公司专注于制造一台类似机器人的“缝纫机”来完成这项工作。
最终，Neuralink希望使BMI的安装过程像Lasik眼科手术一样是非侵入性的，甚至不需要使用全身麻醉剂。马斯克此前曾谈到，BMI需要一种类似Lasik的自动化过程，以克服需要使用训练有素的神经外科医生所涉及的限制和成本。但根据马斯克的说法，这还没有准备好炫耀。马斯克在推特上回答关于这一活动的后续问题时写道：“距离LASIK还很远，但几年后可能会变得相当接近。”
许多科学家对马斯克参与这一医学领域表示欢迎，因为BMI在帮助瘫痪个人和患有神经疾病的人方面具有巨大的潜力。其他人则警告说，他关于这些设备未来效用的说法远未得到证实，他的进展时间表过于乐观。Neuralink去年表示，将在2020年底之前开始临床试验，但没有就这一目标提供任何进一步的更新。
Neuralink尚未正式宣布你将如何观看周五的活动，但密切关注该公司的YouTube频道似乎是一个安全的赌注。
note_en:
Elon Musk  has said that his secretive neurotech firm Neuralink will demonstrate a working “device,” presumably a brain-machine interface, at 6PM ET on Friday. Musk has spoken repeatedly about his belief that BMI devices are needed to help humans keep up with AI by supplementing our brainpower, but right now, his goal is much simpler: to create an implantable device that lets people control phones or computers with their mind.
 Musk initially announced the August 28th “ progress update” back in July, and has now offered more details on what will be shown. He says the update will include the unveiling of a  second-generation robot designed to attach the company’s technology to the brain, and a demo of neurons “ firing in real-time,” though it’s not clear exactly what is meant by this.
 Even compared to Musk’s other ventures like Tesla and SpaceX,  Neuralink is ambitious. The company wants to connect to the brain using flexible electrodes thinner than a human hair that it calls “threads.” Current BMI devices use stiff electrodes for this job, which can cause damage. But inserting flexible electrodes is a much more delicate and challenging task, hence the company’s focus on building a “sewing machine” like robot to do the job.
  Eventually, Neuralink hopes to make the installation process for BMIs as non-invasive as Lasik eye surgery, even removing the need to use general anesthetic. Musk has  previously spoken about the need for an automated Lasik-like process for BMIs to overcome the constraints and costs involved with needing to use highly trained neural surgeons. But this isn’t ready to be shown off yet, according to Musk. “Still far from LASIK, but could get pretty close in a few years,”  Musk tweeted in response to a followup question about the event.
 Many scientists have welcomed Musk’s involvement in this medical field, because of the  huge potential of BMIs to help paralyzed individuals and those with neurological disorders. Others have cautioned that his claims for the future utility of these devices are far from proven, and his timescales for progress overly optimistic. Neuralink  said last year it would start clinical trials by the end of 2020, but has not given any further updates on this goal.
 Neuralink is yet to officially announce how you’ll be able to watch Friday’s event, but keeping an eye on the  company’s YouTube channel seems like a safe bet.
title: 埃隆·马斯克承诺周五演示一款工作正常的Neuralink设备
title_en: Elon Musk promises demo of a working Neuralink device on Friday

BabbleLabs有一套非常特殊的技能。它使用人工智能来增强说话的声音，同时过滤掉无论何时你碰巧在开会时似乎都会出现的不想要的背景噪音。
足够有趣的是，思科还认为这是一种通过删除背景对话而进行的隐私游戏。思科安全和应用业务部高级副总裁兼总经理Jeetu Patel表示，这将大大改善思科用户的会议体验。
帕特尔在一份声明中说：“他们的技术将为我们的客户提供另一项重要的创新--自动消除不需要的噪音--继续提供卓越的Webex会议体验。”帕特尔在最近加入思科之前曾在Box工作多年。
BabbleLabs首席执行官兼联合创始人克里斯·罗文(Chris Rowen)在一篇博客文章中写道，关于被思科收购的对话最近才开始，这笔交易很快就达成了。他写道：“我们很快达成共识，认为将BabbleLabs并入思科协作团队可以极大地加速我们的共同愿景。”
BabbleLabs成立于三年前，根据Crunchbase的数据，它筹集了1800万美元，它有一个有趣但技术性很强的想法。这有时很难转化为可行的商业产品，但对于思科这样的公司来说，这是一个极具吸引力的收购目标。
CRM Essentials创始人兼首席分析师布伦特·利里(Brent Leary)表示，此次收购可以被视为更广泛的行业整合的一部分。他表示：“我们看到整合正在发生，因为大型网络会议参与者正在抢购规模较小的参与者，以完善他们的平台。”
他补充说，“WebEx可能没有得到Zoom那样的关注，但它在企业中的地位仍然很高，这次收购将使他们能够不断改进自己的产品。”
在监管部门批准后，这笔交易预计将在本季度完成。完成后，BabbleLabs员工将成为思科协作小组的一员。
note_en:
BabbleLabs has a very particular set of skills. It uses artificial intelligence to enhance the speaking voice, while filtering out those unwanted background noises that seem to occur whenever you happen to be in a meeting.
 Interestingly enough,  Cisco   also sees this as a kind of privacy play by removing background conversation. Jeetu Patel, senior vice president and general manager in the Cisco Security and Applications Business Unit, says that this should go a long way toward improving the meeting experience for Cisco users.
  “Their technology is going to provide our customers with yet another important innovation — automatically removing unwanted noise — to continue enabling exceptional Webex meeting experiences,” Patel, who was at Box for many years before joining Cisco recently, said in a statement.
 In  a blog post, BabbleLabs CEO and co-founder Chris Rowen wrote that conversations about being acquired by Cisco began just recently, and the deal came together pretty quickly. “We quickly reached a common view that merging  BabbleLabs   into the Cisco Collaboration team could accelerate our common vision dramatically,” he wrote.
 BabbleLabs, which launched three years ago and raised $18 million, according to Crunchbase, had an interesting, but highly technical idea. That can sometimes be difficult to translate into a viable commercial product, but makes a highly attractive acquisition target for a company like Cisco.
 Brent Leary, founder and principal analyst at CRM Essentials, says this acquisition could be seen as part of a broader industry consolidation. “We’re seeing consolidation taking place as the big web conferencing players are snapping up smaller players to round out their platforms,” he said.
 He added, “ WebEx   may not be getting the attention that Zoom is, but it still has a significant presence in the enterprise, and this acquisition will allow them to keep improving their offering,”
 The deal is expected to close in the current quarter after regulatory approval. Upon closing, BabbleLabs employees will become part of Cisco’s Collaboration Group.
title: 思科收购BabbleLabs以过滤视频会议期间割草机的尖叫声
title_en: Cisco acquiring BabbleLabs to filter out the lawn mower screeching during your video conference

自那以后，这款应用重新进入了前十名。但随着虚拟学校在美国的开始，随着孩子们越来越习惯于使用Zoom和Google Meet等替代解决方案，并开始将它们用于课外聊天，这款应用的使用率可能会下降。例如，Meet已经攀升至第15位，因为一些学校在8月份开始上课。Zoom在美国应用商店中排名第一。
新版本的Messenger聊天室现在将在收件箱中聊天选项卡的顶部显示您已被邀请的聊天室，以便您更容易找到它们。通过将选项放在Chats选项卡的前面和中心，还可以更明显地创建新的Room。
其他变化会影响房间管理。Facebook说，你现在可以创建一个带有默认、建议或自定义活动的聊天室，设置未来的开始日期，并定制你的受众选择。此外，通过管理聊天室功能，您可以查看、加入、编辑或稍后删除您创建的聊天室，或者邀请更多人加入现有聊天室。
共享房间选项还可以更容易地向朋友发送通知，提醒他们他们的邀请。
在个性化方面，Messenger开始推出一种新的方式来定制您自己的房间体验。除了之前提供的使用360度背景和情绪照明滤镜的选项外，您现在还可以用自己的照片来装饰房间的背景。
并非巧合的是，房间竞争对手Houseparty最近推出了自己的一项新的个性化功能：边框，这是由独立艺术家设计的原创收藏，用作视频聊天背景。
Facebook今年5月在全球推出了Messenger房间，目的是在冠状病毒大流行期间视频通信越来越受欢迎的情况下，提供社交视频聊天体验。健康危机导致Messenger竞争对手的数量急剧增加，不仅包括继续位居美国应用商店榜首的Zoom，还包括Houseparty、Fam、Bunch等社交视频应用。Z世代的用户也越来越多地在非脸书网站上进行社交活动，包括TikTok，甚至像Roblox和堡垒之夜这样的游戏平台。这意味着Facebook可能会失去对网络效应的控制，因为它与帮助Messenger成为顶级移动应用的Facebook捆绑在一起。
该公司似乎意识到了这种潜在的担忧。本月早些时候，它开始整合Instagram和Messenger的聊天系统，以帮助Messenger保持其顶级应用程序的地位。
note_en:
The app has since re-entered the top 10. But as virtual school begins in the U.S., it could see usage decline as kids grow more comfortable with alternative solutions, like Zoom and Google Meet, and begin to use them for after-school chats, as well. Already, Meet has climbed to No. 15 as some schools started classes in August, for example. And Zoom is No. 1 on the U.S. App Store.
 The new version of Messenger Rooms will now display the Rooms you’ve been invited to up at the top of your Chats tab in your inbox to make them easier to find. It will also make the ability to create a new Room more visible, by placing the option front and center in the Chats tab.
  Other changes impact Rooms management. You can now create a Room with a default, suggested, or custom activity, set a future start date, and customize your audience selection, Facebook says. In addition, the Manage Rooms feature will let you view,  join, edit, or later delete the rooms you have created, or invite more people to join an existing Room.
 A Share Rooms option will also make it easier to send a notification to a friend to remind them of their invite.
  On the personalization front, Messenger is beginning to roll out a new way to customize your own Rooms experience. You’ll now be able to decorate the background of the Room with your own photos, in addition to the previously offered options to use 360-degree backgrounds and mood lighting filters.
 Not coincidentally, Rooms rival  Houseparty recently introduced a new personalization feature of its own: Frames, which are original collections designed by independent artists for use as video chat backgrounds.
 Facebook  rolled out Messenger Rooms globally this May, with the aim of offering a social video chat experience amid growing popularity for video communications seen during the coronavirus pandemic. The health crisis has led to sharp increases for Messenger competitors, including not only Zoom, which continues to sit at the top of the U.S. App Store, but also social video apps like Houseparty, Fam, Bunch and many others. Gen Z users are also increasingly socializing on non-Facebook properties, including TikTok and even gaming platforms like Roblox and Fortnite. That means Facebook could be losing its grip on the network effects by being tied to Facebook that helped make Messenger a top mobile app.
 The company seems to be aware of that potential concern. Earlier this month , it began integrating the chat systems for Instagram and Messenger, to help Messenger retain its top app status.
title: Facebook更新Messenger房间，Zoom位居应用商店首位
title_en: Facebook updates Messenger Rooms as Zoom sits at top of the App Store

除了以无人机闻名外，DJI还在相机稳定市场为自己开辟了一个很好的利基市场。在为航空成像领域开发的技术基础上，该公司推出了一系列产品，从专业视频到消费智能手机配件应有尽有。
今天，这家公司揭开了最新款的面纱。新款Osmo带来了一些新功能和精炼的重塑品牌。OM4最大的升级是它的新附件系统。移动万向节使用永久性粘合剂背衬或滑动附件磁性地卡在手机背面。您的偏好可能取决于您计划使用新配件的频率。
当我们的两位视频编辑去年评论Osmo 3时，他们的主要吹毛求疵是，这款产品有点过于专注于消费者，并没有真正提供他们在这类产品中寻找的那种选择。这是可以理解的。虽然许多专业摄影师都在寻求将智能手机的生产添加到他们的工具箱中，但事实是大疆的主要关注点仍然是消费者。因此，易用性在这里是最重要的，包括单手轻松使用的能力。
除了新的机壳，大多数升级都是新的和升级的拍摄模式。以下是DJI的完整数据：
动态缩放：视觉上吸引人的电影外观，DJI OM 4自动调整缩放功能，以创建阿尔弗雷德·希区柯克(Alfred Hitchcock)流行的戏剧性背景移动。
Timelapse、MotionLapse、HyperLapse：使用Timelapse以加速形式显示时间的流逝；MotionLapse为万向架添加设置的移动点；或HyperLapse以物理方式与万向架一起移动。这三种模式都使用电子稳像(EIS)技术以及三轴万向架来增加平滑度。轻松将这些创意视频分享到社交媒体上，供全世界欣赏。
运动模式：DJI OM4将更快地响应和跟踪主题和动作，以增加场景的动作级别。
全景：从三个富有创意的全景选项中进行选择，以捕捉更广泛的视角，包括3X3全景、240°全景和新的“CloneMe”全景，允许用户将一个人或主题的多个版本添加到一张照片中，以获得独特的创意效果。
旋转镜头万向节运动：在DJI Mimo应用程序中激活并使用操纵杆，万向节将旋转手机以产生旋转效果。
故事模式模板：从其中一个预设模板中选择，为您的内容添加创造性的旋转效果。DJI OM4使用预设的动作、音乐和色调来制作快速视频，非常适合在社交媒体上分享。
ActiveTrack在这里也得到了升级，更容易区分和跟踪一系列不同的主题，包括人和宠物。用户可以使用车载操纵杆在它们之间手动翻转。还有新的手势控制功能。这项功能借鉴了大疆的无人机系列，使用移动来执行自拍等任务。
note_en:
Beyond the drones it’s known for,  DJI   has carved out a nice niche for itself in the camera stabilization market. Building on the technology it has developed for the aerial imaging space, the company’s got a number of products, running the gamut from professional video to consumer smartphone accessories.
  Today the company took the wraps off its latest. The new  Osmo   arrives with a handful of new features and a pithy rebrand. The biggest upgrade for the OM 4 is its new attachment system. The mobile gimbal snaps onto the back of a handset magnetically using a permanent adhesive backing or a slide-on accessory. Your preference will likely be based on how often you plan to use the new accessory.
   When two of our video editors reviewed the Osmo 3 last year, their main quibble was that the product was a bit too heavily focused on consumers and didn’t really offer the kind of options they were looking for in such a product. Understandably so. While a number of pro videographers are looking to add smartphone production to their tool box, the truth is DJI’s main focus continues to be consumers. As such, ease of use is paramount here, including the ability to easily use it with a single hand.
 Beyond the new casing, most of the upgrades are new and upgraded shooting modes. Here’s the full break down, per DJI:
 DynamicZoom: A visually appealing cinematic look, the DJI OM 4 automatically adjusts the zoom function to create the dramatic background shift made popular by Alfred Hitchcock.
 Timelapse,  Motionlapse,  Hyperlapse: Show the passing of time in a sped-up form using TimeLapse; Motionlapse, which adds set movement points for the gimbal; or HyperLapse to physically move with the gimbal. All three modes use Electronic Image Stabilization (EIS) technology as well as the three-axis gimbal for an added level of smoothness. Easily share these creative videos on social media for the world to enjoy.
  Sport Mode: The DJI OM 4 will respond and follow subjects and movements faster to add a level of action to the scene.
 Panoramas : Choose from three creative panorama options to capture a wider perspective, including 3X3 panorama, 240° panorama and a new “CloneMe” panorama, which allows the user to add multiple versions of a person or subject into one shot for a unique and creative effect.
 Spin Shot Gimbal Movement: Activated in the DJI Mimo app and using the joystick, the gimbal will rotate the phone to give a spinning effect.
 Story Mode Templates: Choose from one of the preset templates to add a creative spin to your content. The DJI OM 4 uses preset movements, music and color palates for quick videos perfect for sharing on social media.
  ActiveTrack gets an upgrade as well, here, making it easier to distinguish between and follow a range of different subjects, including people and pets. Users can manually flip between them using the on-board joystick. There’s new gesture control, as well. The feature, borrowed from DJI’s drone line, uses movement to perform tasks like snapping a selfie.
title: 随着磁连接OM4的到来，DJI重新命名了Osmo系列
title_en: DJI rebrands its Osmo line with the arrival of the magnetically connecting OM 4

跳到导航跳跃搜索agōgē(希腊语：阁楼希腊语中的ἀγωγή，或多立克希腊语中的ἀγωγά，agōgā)是要求所有斯巴达男性公民接受的严格的教育和培训计划，除了执政党中的长子Eurypontid和Agiad。训练包括培养对斯巴达人的忠诚度、军事训练(例如，忍受疼痛)、狩猎、跳舞、唱歌和社交(交流)准备。[1]在古希腊语中，“agōgē”一词有多种含义，但在本文中，它通常指的是领导、指导或训练。[2]。
根据民间传说，agōgē是由半虚构的斯巴达立法者莱科格斯引入的，但它的起源被认为是在公元前7世纪到6世纪之间[3][4]，当时国家训练男性公民，从7岁到21岁。[1][5]。
该系统的目的是培养强大而有能力的战士，为斯巴达军队服务。它鼓励顺从和重视斯巴达国家对个人利益的重要性，并催生出未来的斯巴达精英。[1]这些人将成为斯巴达的城墙，因为斯巴达是唯一一座在Lycurgus的命令下被拆除后没有防御墙的希腊城市。[6]纪律很严格，鼓励雄性相互争斗，以确定群体中最强壮的成员。
Agōgē在希腊世界享有盛誉，许多来自其他城市的贵族家庭争先恐后地把他们的儿子送到斯巴达参加agōgē，时间长短不一。斯巴达人非常挑剔他们允许哪些年轻人入学。这样的荣誉通常授予其他城市的斯巴达的普罗克诺伊(πρόξενοι)，以及其他几个最高血统和最重要的家庭。
当一个男孩出生时，他被用酒洗澡，因为他相信这会让他变得强壮。然后，每个婴儿都会由孩子所在部落的格罗西亚人(一个主要的长老斯巴达人理事会)成员进行检查，看看他是否健康到足以让他活下去。如果婴儿没有通过测试，他会被放在塔吉特斯山的底部几天，进行一项测试，结果是暴露在空气中死亡，或者存活下来。7岁时，男童在派多诺莫斯(παιδονόμος)或负责监督教育的地方法官--牧童--的授权下入学。这开始了三个阶段中的第一个阶段：帕迪斯人(大约7-17岁)、Payedískoi人(17-19岁)和hēbōNTE人(20-29岁)。一些经典资料表明，在这些类别中，还存在着按年进一步细分的情况。[1]。
男孩们成群结队地生活在一位年长的男子手下。他们被鼓励将自己的忠诚献给被称为Syssitia的公共食堂，而不是他们的家人。从12岁开始，男孩每年只有一件衣服-一件被称为Phoinikis的红色斗篷(一个地名，反映斗篷中使用或模仿的提利亚紫色染料的腓尼基起源)。他们还用没有刀的手工从欧罗塔斯河拉来的芦苇制成了床。男孩们故意吃不饱饭，以鼓励他们为自己偷食物；然而，普卢塔克表示，如果他们被抓住，他们将被无情地鞭打，并被削减到他们的普通食物津贴。这是为了培养出体格健壮的士兵，而不是胖子。[7]这是为了培养体格健壮的士兵，而不是肥胖的士兵。这让孩子们习惯了饥饿，也避免了饥饿在战斗中成为一个问题。只有两个斯巴达皇室家族(Agiad和Eurypontids)的法定继承人不受这一程序的影响。
在大约12岁的时候，这些男孩会与一名年轻的成年男性斯巴达人建立一种制度化的关系。普卢塔克描述了这种斯巴达式的同龄人关系(性爱关系)，在这种关系中，年纪稍大的战士会让有前途的年轻人与有教育意义的年轻人建立长期的关系。然而，国王阿格西劳斯二世(Agesilaus II)的雅典朋友色诺芬(Xenophon)表示，Lycurgus的法律严格禁止与男孩发生性关系。他的儿子们有幸接受过阿加戈的培训。根据普鲁塔克的说法，年长的斯巴达人在战斗中是他们的队长，在家里是他们的主人。年龄较大的男孩经常被指示收集木头，而身体较弱和身体不好的男孩则收集沙拉和草药等东西。[7]男孩们被期望请求这种关系，这被视为在战场上传递知识和保持忠诚的一种方法。在派迪斯凯伊的阶段，18岁左右，学生们成为斯巴达军队的预备役成员。此外，一些年轻人被允许成为秘密警察的一部分，秘密警察的一种形式，那里的成员被指示监视赫洛特人。他们还会杀死夜间外出的Helot奴隶或SPO
那个时代的教育在斯巴达起到了很好的均衡器的作用。男人注定要在田径和战斗中竞争。帮助者和普通人可能只会根据需要发展他们的阅读和写作技能，以便进行祈祷供奉和阅读重要的铭文。成为国王、外交官或将军的斯巴达人也会提高他们职位所需的修辞、阅读和写作技能。公民男性斯巴达人中的大多数人是如何识字的，或者他们是否识字，这一点并不为人所知。然而，在普鲁塔克的斯巴达妇女格言[8]中提到了战争中母子之间的通信，这表明他们有一定程度的识字能力。
女孩还接受了一种国家教育形式，包括舞蹈、体操和其他运动；还有其他科目，如音乐和诗歌，包括写作和战争教育。[1]女孩们在接受教育期间都是由母亲在家抚养长大的。[9]优雅和修养等特质不受欢迎，而偏向于身体健康和品德高尚。女孩们还被鼓励通过在公共场合羞辱男性和批评他们的锻炼来帮助男性。就像斯巴达的男性被培养成战士一样，斯巴达的女性也接受了主要任务的训练：孕育战士；俗话说，只有斯巴达的女性才能生出男人。女孩们被鼓励变得强壮和健康，她们参加了体育比赛，骑着露肩的石鳖跑脚步。(译者注：斯巴达的女性被培养成勇士，而斯巴达的女性则接受训练，培养成勇士。俗话说，只有斯巴达的女性才能生出男人。)女孩们鼓励自己强壮健康，参加体育比赛，骑着露肩的石鳖跑步。
斯巴达妇女穿着旁边敞开的老式圆顶(πέπλος)，引起了其他希腊人的嘲笑，这些希腊人称她们为“大腿淋浴”(phainomērídes(φαινομηρίδες))。在宗教仪式、节日和体育锻炼期间，女孩和女人都是裸体的。[11]。
任何没有成功通过阿格格的男性都被剥夺了斯巴达公民身份。在不同时期，这种选择过程被认为对斯巴达社会不利，特别是在自由的斯巴达男性公民数量减少(寡人)的情况下。这一做法在公元前3世纪衰落，但在公元前220年代的某个时候被克利奥梅尼三世成功地复兴。然而，不到40年后，公元前188年，哲学家废除了这一做法。[12]阿戈奇在公元前146年罗马人在阿契亚战争中击败阿契亚人后恢复[13]，虽然形式比原来的要小。
罗马氏族仅限于14至19岁的男性，本质上是麻疯族，由族系(公民部落)组织。教学内容包括田径、歌唱、舞蹈、军事，可能还有一些学术训练。这些学生由名为Bideioi(#34；监督员)和一名监护人(#34；法律监护人)的官员监督。[1]在弗拉维安王朝，一种以团队为基础的结构被引入罗马教区，这种结构将学生群体置于一名组长或野狗(βοαγός)的指挥之下。一些负担不起培训费用的贫困学生可以得到赞助。[1]。
^a b c d e f g h i Hodkinson，Stephen(1996)。再往前走。在霍恩布洛尔，西蒙(编辑)。牛津古典词典。牛津大学出版社。
奥利弗·戈德史密斯(1836)。平诺克的改进版的戈德史密斯博士的希腊历史。钥匙和比德尔。第76页。
“肯塔基州普里”(2012年)。雅典和斯巴达妇女的社会和政治角色。军刀和卷轴。1：6-14，归档时间为2017-05-13，原件存档时间为2017-05-13。
^Chrimes，K.M.T.(1999)。古斯巴达：重新审视证据。曼彻斯特大学出版社。第46-48页。ISBN978-0719057410。
弗朗西斯·凯恩斯(2006)。塞克斯图斯·普罗提乌斯：奥古斯都的雅俗主义者。剑桥大学出版社。第379页。ISBN978-0521864572-通过谷歌图书。

Jump to navigation  Jump to search  The   agōgē ( Greek:  ἀγωγή in  Attic Greek, or  ἀγωγά,  agōgā in  Doric Greek) was the rigorous education and training program mandated for all male  Spartan citizens, except for the firstborn son in the ruling houses,  Eurypontid and  Agiad. The training involved cultivating loyalty to the Spartan group, military training (e.g., pain tolerance), hunting, dancing, singing, and social (communicating) preparation.  [1] The word  agōgē had various meaning in  ancient Greek, but in this context it generally meant leading, guidance, or training.  [2]
 According to folklore,  agōgē was introduced by the semi-mythical Spartan law-giver  Lycurgus but its origins are thought to be between the 7th and 6th centuries BC  [3]  [4] when the state trained male citizens from the ages of seven to twenty-one.  [1]  [5]
 The aim of the system was to produce strong and capable warriors to serve the  Spartan army. It encouraged  conformity and the importance of the Spartan state over one's personal interest and generated the future elites of Sparta.  [1] The men would become the "walls of Sparta" because Sparta was the only  Greek city with no defensive walls after they had been demolished at the order of  Lycurgus.  [6] Discipline was strict and the males were encouraged to fight amongst themselves to determine the strongest member of the group.
 The  agōgē was prestigious throughout the Greek world, and many aristocratic families from other cities vied to send their sons to Sparta to participate in the  agōgē for varying periods of time. The Spartans were very selective in which young men they would permit to enroll. Such honors were usually awarded to the   próxenoi ( πρόξενοι) of Sparta in other cities and to a few other families of supreme ancestry and importance.
   When a boy was born, he was washed with wine in the belief that this would make him strong. Every infant was then examined by members of the   Gerousia (a council of leading elder Spartans) from the child's tribe to see whether he was fit and healthy enough to be allowed to live. In the event that the baby did not pass the test, he was placed at the base of  Mount Taygetus for several days for a test that ended with death by exposure, or survival. At the age of seven, the male child was enrolled in the  agoge under the authority of the  paidonómos ( παιδονόμος), or "boy-herder", a  magistrate charged with supervising education. This began the first of the three stages of the  agoge: the  paídes (about ages 7–17), the  paidískoi (ages 17–19), and the  hēbōntes (ages 20–29). Some classical sources indicate that there were further subdivisions by year within these classes.  [1]
 The boys lived in groups ( agélai, "herds") under an older man. They were encouraged to give their loyalty to their communal  mess hall known as the   Syssitia, rather than to their families. Beginning at the age of 12 boys would be given only one item of clothing per year—a red cloak known as a  Phoinikis (a  toponym reflecting the  Phoenician origin of the  Tyrian purple dye used or imitated in the cloak). They also created beds out of reeds pulled by hand, with no knife, from the  Eurotas River. Boys were intentionally underfed to encourage them to steal food for themselves; however, Plutarch states that "if they were caught they would be mercilessly whipped and reduced to their ordinary food allowance."  [7] This was meant to produce well-built soldiers rather than fat ones. This let the boys become accustomed to hunger, and this prevented hunger from being a problem during battle. Only the  heirs apparent of the two Spartan royal households (the  Agiads and  Eurypontids) were exempt from the process.
 At around age 12 the boys would enter into an institutionalized relationship with a young adult male Spartan.  Plutarch described this form of  Spartan pederasty (erotic relationship) wherein somewhat older warriors would engage promising youths in a long-lasting relationship with an instructive motive. However  Xenophon, an Athenian friend of King  Agesilaus II and whose sons were given the honour of training in the Agoge, comments that the laws of  Lycurgus strictly prohibited sexual relationships with the boys. According to Plutarch, the older Spartans were their Captains during combat and their master at home. The older boys were often instructed to collect wood, while the weaker and unfit boys gathered things such as salad and herbs.  [7] The boys were expected to request the relationship, which was seen as a method to pass on knowledge and maintain loyalty on the battlefield. At the stage of  paidiskoi, around the age of 18, the students became reserve members of the Spartan army. Also, some youths were allowed to become part of the  Crypteia, a type of 'Secret Police', where the members were instructed to spy on the  Helot population. They would also kill Helot slaves who were out at night or spoke about rebelling against the Government, to help keep the population submissive. The state supported this by formally declaring war on the Helots every autumn. This meant killing a Helot was not regarded as a crime, but a valuable deed for the good of the state.  [1]
 At the stage of  hēbōntes, roughly age 20, the students became fully part of the   syssitia and Spartan army, although they continued to live in  barracks and to compete for a place among the Spartan   hippeis, the royal guard of  honor.  [1] At the age of 20, students were voted into one of the public messes. The voting was done by Spartan peers who were members of the mess and must be unanimous. Rejected candidates could try to gain entry to a different mess for up to ten years. If a man failed to gain entry into a mess by age 30, he would not gain full Spartan citizenship. At the age of 30, men were permitted to marry and to become full citizens of Sparta who could vote and hold office.
 Education in the  agoge served as a great equalizer in Sparta. Men were meant to compete in athletics and in battle. Helots and common men likely only developed their reading and writing skills as needed to make votive offerings and read important inscriptions. Spartans who became kings, diplomats or generals would also improve the rhetoric, reading and writing skills necessary for their positions. How the majority of the population of citizen male Spartans became literate, or whether they were literate at all, is not well known. However, there is reference made in Plutarch's "Sayings of Spartan Women"  [8] to correspondence kept between mother and sons at war, which would suggest some degree of literacy.
  Girls also had a form of state education involving  dance,  gymnastics and other sports; together with other subjects such as music and poetry, including writing and war education.  [1] Girls were raised at home by their mothers while they were being educated.  [9] Traits such as grace and culture were frowned upon in favor of high physical fitness and moral integrity. The girls were also encouraged to help the males by humiliating them in public and by criticizing their exercising. Just as Spartan males were raised to become warriors, the females of Sparta were trained for their primary task: giving birth to warriors; as the saying went, "only Spartan women could give birth to men."  [10] Encouraged to be strong and healthy, girls participated in athletic competitions, running footraces in off-the-shoulder  chitons.
 Spartan women wore the old-fashioned  peplos ( πέπλος), open at the side, leading to banter at their expense among the other Greeks who dubbed them  phainomērídes ( φαινομηρίδες) the "thigh-showers." At religious ceremonies, on holidays and during physical exercise girls and women were nude.  [11]
  Any male who did not successfully pass through the  agoge was denied Spartan citizenship. At various times this selection process was seen as detrimental to Spartan society, particularly when the number of free male Spartan citizens dwindled (oliganthropia). The practice waned in the 3rd century BC but was successfully reinvigorated some time in the 220s BC by  Cleomenes III. It was abolished, however, less than forty years later by  Philopoemen in 188 BC.  [12] The  agoge was reinstated in the year 146 BC after the Romans defeated the  Achaeans in the  Achaean War,  [13] albeit in a lesser form than the original.
  The Roman  agoge was limited to males between the ages of 14 to 19 and was essentially  ephebic in nature and organized by   phyles (citizen tribes). The instruction consisted of athletics, singing, dancing, military and probably some academic training. The students were supervised by officials called  bideioi ("overseers") and a   patronomos ("guardian of law").  [1] During the  Flavian dynasty a team-based structure was introduced to the Roman  agoge which put groups of students under the command of a team leader or  boagos (βοαγός). Sponsorship was available to some poor students who could not afford the training.  [1]

     ^   Goldsmith, Oliver (1836).  Pinnock's Improved Edition of Dr. Goldsmith's History of Greece. Key and Biddle. p. 76.
    ^   O'Pry, Kay (2012).  "Social and Political Roles of Women in Athens and Sparta".  Saber and Scroll.  1: 6–14. Archived from  the original on 2017-05-13 .
  ^   Chrimes, K.M.T. (1999).  Ancient Sparta: A Re-examination of the Evidence. Manchester University Press. pp. 46–48.  ISBN   978-0719057410.
 ^   Cairns, Francis (2006).  Sextus Propertius: The Augustan Elegist. Cambridge University Press. p. 379.  ISBN   978-0521864572 – via Google Books.

键盘是怎么工作的？除了在花哨的游戏键盘上，你为什么不能同时按三个键的任意组合呢？这意味着关于如何检测/编码按键。
键是如何解锁的？有没有模拟逻辑，或者键盘上有没有微控制器来做这件事，或者别的什么？薄膜开关是如何工作的？
如何通知操作系统按键？我大概可以用286回答这个问题，但是现在它以某种方式通过x2APIC完成了，对吗？这是怎么运作的？
另外，USB、PS/2和AT键盘有什么不同吗？USB是如何工作的？那么笔记本电脑键盘呢？那只是一个USB连接吗？
USB接口如何工作？你有一个可以处理10 Gb/s的连接。如果正在连接的物理小玩意儿之间有任何差距，那肯定不会起作用。人们如何设计既能承受数万次插入又能保持公差的连接器呢？
操作系统如何告诉程序发生了什么事？它怎么知道该跟哪个节目对话呢？
浏览器如何知道要尝试加载网页？我猜它看到的是一个&#34；http://&#34；，或者只是假设任何没有前缀的东西都是一个URL？
假设我们没有缓存网页，所以我们必须执行DNS查询等操作。
DNS是如何工作的？DNS缓存是如何工作的？让我们假设它没有缓存在附近的任何地方，我们必须去找一些遥远的DNS服务器。
TCP？我们建立联系了吗？我们是针对DNS执行此操作，还是必须使用UDP？
操作系统如何决定是否应该允许传出连接？如果有软件防火墙怎么办？这是怎么运作的？
对于TCP，没有TLS/SSL，我们可以只进行慢启动，然后执行一些标准的拥塞协议，对吗？这里面有没有更深层次的复杂性呢？
不管怎么说，网卡怎么知道该怎么做呢？有没有一个我们写入的内存区域，网卡可以看到，或者它只是直接监控总线事务？
好的，假设有一个存储区。这是怎么运作的？我们怎么写记忆呢？
CPU/SoC中发生了一些事情！这是我知道一些东西的少数几个领域之一，所以，我将跳过这一点。一个信号最终会在一些引脚上发出。那是什么信号？现在，人们使用DDR3，但我们并不总是使用该协议。据推测，DDR3让我们比DDR2更快，DDR2比DDR更快，以此类推，但为什么呢？
然后信号最终进入DRAM模块。与CPU一样，我将基本上忽略内部发生的事情，但我很好奇DRAM模块是仍然是沟槽电容器还是堆叠式电容器，或者这项技术已经向前发展了吗？
回到我们的网卡，当信号在线路上发出时会发生什么？为什么100Mb以太网需要5类而不是3类电缆？这纯粹是信号完整性的问题，还是电缆实际上有不同的布线？
低于该水平的导线肯定足够长，它们可以起到传输线/波导的作用。如何处理终止？双绞线是否足以防止电感耦合，还是有更多奇特的东西在发生？
假设我们有到电缆调制解调器的本地以太网连接。电缆调制解调器是如何工作的？电缆是在不同客户之间以某种方式复用的吗？如何通过一根同轴电缆获得如此大的带宽？
回到上一层，电缆连接最终到达ISP。ISP如何知道将数据路由到何处？Internet路由是如何工作的？报头中的某些位决定路由？如何调整路由表？
另外，8.8.8.8的DNS内容是任播的，对吗？这与路由正常流量有何不同？Cloudflare CDN提供的任何服务也是如此。他们需要做些什么来防止路由抖动和其他不良情况？
斯坦福/Coursera算法课程IIRC提到，它基本上是一种分布式的贝尔曼-福特(Bellman-Ford)计算。但是，是什么阻止了人们建立虚假的路线呢？
如果我们能弄清楚从ISP到哪里去，我们的数据包就会通过一些边缘路由器、一些核心路由器、另一个边缘路由器，然后通过它们的网络进入数据中心的“肉”。
一定有某种激光。哪种？信号是如何调制的？是WDM还是TDM？是单模光纤还是多模光纤？
如果它是WDM，它是如何复用/解复用的？在自由空间里放棱镜会很奇怪，对吧？这是AWG可以做的事情。那是实际使用的东西吗？
链路之间必须有中继器。中继器是如何工作的？他们是只是增强信号，还是先解码信号以避免传播噪音？如果是后者，中继器之间必须有DCF。
能增强信号的东西是最简单的情况。掺铒光纤放大器是如何工作的？它基本上只是通过掺杂光纤传输电流，还是有更深层次的原因？
低于这一水平，就有标准单模光纤和DCF如何工作的问题。
不管怎么说，我们为什么需要贴现现金流呢？我想标准光纤和DCF的组合要比色散很低的光纤便宜。为什么会这样呢？
纤维是如何工作的呢？我的意思是，好吧，这可能是一种波导，它使用不同的介质来控制光线，但是好的光纤和坏的光纤有什么不同呢？
例如，在过去的几十年里，光纤有没有改变来严重降低偏振模色散？那件事怎么可能？这只是更精确的制造，还是涉及到其他东西？
回到上几层，我们进入一个数据中心。上面有什么？我们的数据包通过交换网络传输到机器？可能的交换机拓扑是什么？Facebook并不是直接从Dally and Towles中走出来的东西，但它是你可以想象到的那种用这种知识建立起来的东西。自从FB发布他们的拓扑结构以来，时间还不够长，人们可以复制它们，但是这个想法是否足够明显，以至于你会认为它是独立复制的？
等等，这是对的吗？我们是否应该期望DNS服务器位于某个数据中心的某个位置？
在任何情况下，在所有这些之后，我们的DNS将查询解析到一个IP。我们建立联系，然后呢？
然后我们拿回一些文件，浏览器必须以某种方式呈现它们。有HTML的请求，还有CSS和js的请求，还有单独的图像请求吗？这一定很复杂，因为浏览器很复杂。我不知道这件事有多复杂，所以我肯定遗漏了很多东西。
在浏览器渲染之后，它是如何到达GPU的，GPU做了什么？
对于2D图形，我们可能只需通知操作系统...。某物。这是怎么运作的？
操作系统如何与GPU对话？有没有一些内存映射区域，您只能在其中绘制像素，或者它是否比这更复杂？
LCD显示器是如何工作的？显示器和GPU之间的连接是如何工作的？
但是，如果网站上有广告，那不是很复杂吗？定向广告和广告拍卖是如何运作的？可能在200毫秒内发生了一堆事情？
note_en:
How does a keyboard work? Why can’t you press an arbitrary combination of three keys at once, except on fancy gaming keyboards? That implies something about how key presses are detected/encoded.
  How are keys debounced? Is there some analog logic, or is there a microcontroller in the keyboard that does this, or what? How do membrane switches work?
  How is the OS notified of the keypress? I could probably answer this for a 286, but nowadays it's somehow done through x2APIC, right? How does that work?
  Also, USB, PS/2, and AT keyboards are different, somehow? How does USB work? And what about laptop keyboards? Is that just a USB connection?
  How does a USB connector work? You have this connection that can handle 10Gb/s. That surely won't work if there's any gap at all between the physical doodads that are being connected. How do people design connectors that can withstand tens of thousands of insertions and still maintain their tolerances?
  How does the OS tell the program something happened? How does it know which program to talk to?
  How does the browser know to try to load a webpage? I guess it sees an "http://" or just assumes that anything with no prefix is a URL?
  Assume we don't have the webpage cached, so we have to do DNS queries and stuff.
  How does DNS work? How does DNS caching work? Let's assume it isn't cached at anywhere nearby and we have to go find some far away DNS server.
  TCP? We establish a connection? Do we do that for DNS or does it have to be UDP?
  How does the OS decide if an outgoing connection should be allowed? What if there's a software firewall? How does that work?
  For TCP, without TLS/SSL, we can just do slow-start followed by some standard congestion protocol, right? Is there some deeper complexity there?
    For what matter, how does the network card know what to do? Is there a memory region we write to that the network card can see or does it just monitor bus transactions directly?
  Ok, say there's a memory region. How does that work? How do we write memory?
  Some things happen in the CPU/SoC! This is one of the few areas where I know something, so, I'll skip over that. A signal eventually comes out on some pins. What's that signal? Nowadays, people use DDR3, but we didn't always use that protocol. Presumably DDR3 lets us go faster than DDR2, which was faster than DDR, and so on, but why?
  And then the signal eventually goes into a DRAM module. As with the CPU, I'm going to mostly ignore what's going on inside, but I'm curious if DRAM modules still either trench capacitors or stacked capacitors, or has this technology moved on?
  Going back to our network card, what happens when the signal goes out on the wire? Why do you need a cat5 and not a cat3 cable for 100Mb Ethernet? Is that purely a signal integrity thing or do the cables actually have different wiring?
  One level below that the wires are surely long enough that they can act like transmission lines / waveguides. How is termination handled? Is twisted pair sufficient to prevent inductive coupling or is there more fancy stuff going on?
  Say we have a local Ethernet connection to a cable modem. How do cable modems work? Isn't cable somehow multiplexed between different customers? How is it possible to get so much bandwidth through a single coax cable?
  Going back up a level, the cable connection eventually gets to the ISP. How does the ISP know where to route things? How does internet routing work? Some bits in the header decide the route? How do routing tables get adjusted?
  Also, the 8.8.8.8 DNS stuff is anycast, right? How is that different from routing "normal" traffic? Ditto for anything served from a Cloudflare CDN. What do they need to do to prevent route flapping and other badness?
    IIRC, the Stanford/Coursera algorithms course mentioned that it's basically a distributed Bellman-Ford calculation. But what prevents someone from putting bogus routes up?
  If we can figure out where to go our packets go from our ISP through some edge router, some core routers, another edge router, and then go through their network to get into the “meat” of a datacenter.
      There must be some kind of laser. What kind? How is the signal modulated? Is it WDM or TDM? Is it single-mode or multi-mode fiber?
  If it's WDM, how is it muxed/demuxed? It would be pretty weird to have a prism in free space, right? This is the kind of thing an AWG could do. Is that what's actually used?
  There must be repeaters between links. How do repeaters work? Do they just boost the signal or do they decode it first to avoid propagating noise? If the latter, there must be DCF between repeaters.
  Something that just boosts the signal is the simplest case. How does an EDFA work? Is it basically just running current through doped fiber, or is there something deeper going on there?
  Below that level, there's the question of how standard single mode fiber and DCF work.
  Why do we need DCF, anyway? I guess it's cheaper to have a combination of standard fiber and DCF than to have fiber with very low dispersion. Why is that?
  How does fiber even work? I mean, ok, it's probably a waveguide that uses different dielectrics to keep the light contained, but what's the difference between good fiber and bad fiber?
  For example, hasn't fiber changed over the past couple decades to severely reduce PMD? How is that possible? Is that just more precise manufacturing, or is there something else involved?
  Going back up a few levels, we go into a datacenter. What's up there? Our packets go through a switching network to TOR to machine? What's a likely switch topology?  Facebook's isn't quite something straight out of  Dally and Towles, but it's the kind of thing you could imagine building with that kind of knowledge. It hasn't been long enough since FB published their topology for people to copy them, but is the idea obvious enough that you'd expect it to be independently "copied"?
  Wait, is that even right? Should we expect a DNS server to sit somewhere in some datacenter?
  In any case, after all this our DNS resolves query to an IP. We establish a connection, and then what?
    And then we get some files back and the browser has to render them somehow. There's a request for the HTML and also for the CSS and js, and separate requests for images? This must be complicated, since browsers are complicated. I don't have any idea of the complexity of this, so there must be a lot I'm missing.
  After the browser renders something, how does it get to the GPU and what does the GPU do?
  For 2d graphics, we probably just notify the OS of... something. How does that work?
  And how does the OS talk to the GPU? Is there some memory mapped region where you can just paint pixels, or is it more complicated than that?
  How does an LCD display work? How does the connection between the monitor and the GPU work?
      But if the site has ads, isn't that stuff pretty complicated? How do targeted ads and ad auctions work? A bunch of stuff somehow happens in maybe 200ms?

note:
当Lucid Motors透露其即将推出的电动轿车的续航里程时，人们大吃一惊。独立测试证实，一辆将于今年晚些时候投入生产的Lucid Air一次充电可行驶517英里(832公里)。然而，当时我们并不知道这辆测试车的电池容量，因为Lucid之前曾表示Air将提供从60kWh到120kWh的电池包。周三早上，我们得到了答案：113千瓦时。
对于那些希望入门级机型(预计起价约为6万美元)能有这么长腿的人来说，这可能会让他们有点失望。但是距离113千瓦时517英里是4.57英里/千瓦时(13.6千瓦时/100公里)，按照电动汽车的标准，这仍然是非常有效的，对于一辆60千瓦时的Air来说，这相当于高达274英里(440公里)的续航里程。
此前，Lucid曾表示，它正在计划60kWh，100kWh和120kWh的空气包。如果这些数字是总千瓦时，而不是可用千瓦时(这始终是一个较低的数字，以防止电池完全放电)，并且扩展射程电池组的总容量为120kWh(可用容量为113kWh)，则60kWh Air可能有可用56.5kWh，应等于258英里/415公里)。
Lucid首席执行官兼首席技术官彼得·罗林森表示，真正的魔力在于公司如何包装电动总成。
通过逐渐增加电池来获得更大的续航里程相对容易，但获得愚蠢的续航里程会增加重量和成本，并减少内部空间。Lucid Air通过其内部技术实现了非凡的续航里程，同时还减少了电池尺寸，从而实现了整体车辆级效率的突破。Lucid的突破不仅仅是几个百分点；我们正在讨论的是一个重大的改进，我将在9月9日进一步报道这一点。他在发给ARS的一份新闻稿中表示：这代表着一个真正的突破，也是我在未来以及Lucid在推进电气化和可持续机动性方面所扮演的角色最令我兴奋的地方。
9月9日的发布应该会填补关于Air的剩余空白，包括各种不同规格的确切价格。
在其他Lucid新闻中，该公司的下一款车现在已经被看到在公共场合进行测试。事实上，推特用户@mvhead一周前就发现了这辆车，他在一条关闭拍照的道路上遇到了一辆Lucid Air和一辆风格相似的SUV：
我刚开车经过佩斯卡德罗，碰见了一张“清醒”的照片。他们用CHP封锁了Lucid Air&Amp；Lucid SUV的道路。
Lucid目前对这款SUV只字不提，但它似乎极有可能会与Air分享很多东西。
note_en:
When  Lucid Motors revealed the range of its forthcoming electric sedan, plenty of eyebrows were raised. Independent testing confirmed that a Lucid Air,  which enters production later this year, traveled 517 miles (832km) on a single charge. At the time, however, we didn't know the battery capacity of that test vehicle, since Lucid has previously stated that the Air would be available with packs varying from 60kWh to 120kWh. On Wednesday morning, we got our answer: 113kWh.
 That might come as a little disappointment to those who were hoping that the entry-level model (which is expected to start at approximately $60,000) would have such long legs. But 517 miles from 113kWh is 4.57 miles/kWh (13.6kWh/100km), which is still extremely efficient by EV standards and would equate to up to 274 miles (440km) of range for an Air with a 60kWh pack.
 Previously Lucid has said it's planning 60kWh, 100kWh, and 120kWh packs for the Air. If those numbers are total kWh as opposed to useable kWh (which is always a lower number to prevent the battery from completely discharging) and the total capacity of the extended range pack is 120kWh (for a usable capacity of 113kWh) then a 60kWh Air would probably have a usable 56.5kWh, which should equate to 258 miles/415km).
 Lucid CEO and CTO Peter Rawlinson says that the real magic is how the company is able to package the electric powertrain.
 "It's relatively easy to achieve more range by adding progressively more batteries, but gaining 'dumb range' that way increases weight and cost, and reduces interior space. Lucid Air has achieved its remarkable range whilst also reducing battery size through its in-house technology, resulting in a breakthrough in overall vehicle-level efficiency. Lucid’s breakthrough is not merely just a few percent; we are talking about a significant improvement, which I shall cover further on September 9th. This represents a true breakthrough and what excites me the most for the future and for Lucid’s role in advancing electrification and sustainable mobility," he said in a press release sent to Ars.
 That September 9 launch should fill in the remaining blanks about the Air, including exact prices for the various different specifications.
 In other Lucid news, the company's next vehicle has now been seen testing in public. In fact, it was spotted over a week ago by Twitter user @mvahead,  who came across a Lucid Air and a similarly styled SUV on a road that had been closed for a photoshoot:
 I was just drive through Pescadero and came across a Lucid shoot. They blocked off the road with CHP for the Lucid Air & Lucid SUV
 Lucid is saying nothing about this SUV right now, but it seems extremely likely it will share a lot with the Air.
title: 517英里射程的Lucid Air将使用113千瓦时的电池组
title_en: That 517-mile-range Lucid Air will use a 113kWh battery pack

note:
当我有机会在“荒原3”中播放InXile的末日后RPG的预发行副本时，我欣然接受了。作为RPG的铁杆粉丝和《荒原2》的支持者，为我的工作找到机会打下一场系列赛显然是一件轻而易举的事情。对于那些还不熟悉这一系列的人来说，这是一部黑色幽默的战术斗士，背景是一个另类的宇宙，后世界末日时代的美国，被分成了广泛分离的封地和无序的混乱。
如果你像Fallout一样思考，但又是以轮流为基础的，那么你就错了--“荒原”和“Fallout”版的二战后美国有很多相似之处，包括一些滑稽的复古未来主义元素。“荒原”和“Fallout”版的二战后美国有很多相似之处，包括一些滑稽的复古未来主义元素。“荒原”和“Fallout”版的二战后美国有很多相似之处，包括一些滑稽的复古未来主义气息。但是，Fallout的世界似乎是从50年代末兴起的，而荒原在80年代的某个地方建立了分支机构。HUD包括一个阿尔卑斯山风格的卡带平台，带有令人讨厌的图形均衡器，俱乐部有DiscoBot，CPU超频到66 MHz，等等。
荒原也试图比Fallout更严肃地对待自己-它的幽默不那么夸张，它的伦理选择更困难，它更频繁地试图让你感受到它的人物所处困境的严重性，直到并包括残忍的谋杀和吃人。这是一条很好的路线，但戏谑的文化背景和愚蠢的笑话让玩家在游戏中角色所面临的可怕情况下不会陷入绝望。
这可能是一个黑暗的世界，有时也是一次艰难的经历，但到目前为止，我们已经享受了40多个小时的荒原3航海之旅。为此，我们在从这里开始的这篇评论中努力避免剧情剧透。继续读下去，不要担心毁了你自己的后世界末日之旅。
note_en:
When I got the chance to play a pre-release copy of inXile's post-apocalyptic RPG  Wasteland 3, I jumped at it. As a huge fan of RPGs in general and a backer of  Wasteland 2, getting to play the next game in the series  for my job was an obvious no-brainer. For those who aren't already familiar with the series, it's a darkly humorous tactical battler, set in an alternate-universe post-apocalyptic America divided into widely separated fiefdoms and sprawling chaos.
 If you're thinking "like  Fallout, but turn-based?" you're not too far off—there are a lot of similarities between  Wasteland's and  Fallout's versions of post-WWIII America, including some hilariously retrofuturistic touches. But where  Fallout's world seems to have sprung from the late '50s,  Wasteland's setting branches out from somewhere in the '80s. The HUD includes an Alpine-style cassette deck with obnoxious graphic equalizer, the clubs have Discobots, the CPUs are "overclocked to 66MHz," and so on.
 Wasteland tries to take itself a little more seriously than  Fallout does, too—its humor is a little less over the top, its ethical choices are harder, and it tries more frequently to get you to feel the gravity of the plights its characters find themselves in, up to and including brutal murder and cannibalism. It's a fine line to walk, but the tongue-in-cheek cultural references and silly jokes keep the player from slipping into despair at the awful situations faced by the game's characters.
 It may be a dark world and a trying experience at times, but we've enjoyed 40+ hours of navigating  Wasteland 3 so far. To that end, we're strenuously avoiding plot spoilers in this review from here. Read on without fear of spoiling your own post-apocalyptic journey ahead.
title: “荒原3印象：世界末日后的策略、伦理学和经济学”
title_en: Wasteland 3 impressions: Post-apocalyptic tactics, ethics, and economics

note:
Desktop Metal‘s在其存在的五年中不乏投资者对其业务的兴趣。到目前为止，这家金属3D打印公司已经筹集了4.3亿美元，成为在这一过程中获得独角兽地位最快的美国公司之一。
今天，它也成为最近一系列宣布打算上市的公司中最新的一家。
这家位于马萨诸塞州伯灵顿的公司计划通过与Trine Acquisition Corp.合并在纽约证券交易所(New York Stock Exchange)上市，股票代码为“DM”。这家黑色支票公司去年3月宣布了自己的首次公开募股(IPO)，募集资金2.61亿美元。
根据这项新协议，Trine将与Desktop InMetal合并，据其自己估计，将创建一家价值高达25亿美元的公司。Desktop Metal去年年初的最新估值为15亿美元。因此，由SPAC牵头的这笔交易可能会为桌面金属投资者提供一个有吸引力的加价。
在这笔交易之前，包括维珍银河在内的SPAC并购案最近有所增加(总体上出现了一系列活动)。
根据一份描述这笔交易的新闻稿，由SPAC牵头的首次公开募股将产生高达575美元的“总收益”。Trine将出资3亿美元，另有2.75亿美元来自“承诺的普通股管道，每股10.00美元”。术语PIPE代表“私募股权投资”，允许更多资金在需要时流入SPAC牵头的交易。
除了进一步加强自己的研发努力外，Desktop Metal还计划进行自己的收购，称这是对添加剂制造/3D打印行业的“建设性整合”。这笔交易将使Desktop Metal成为其所认为的“加法制造2.0”的主要参与者-本质上是3D打印创新的第二波浪潮，在经历了数年和数十年的炒作之后，这项技术最终可能会发挥其真正颠覆制造业的潜力。
创始人兼首席执行官里克·富洛普(Rick Fulop)在一份新闻稿中表示：“我们正处于采用添加剂制造的重大转折点，桌面金属公司(Desktop Metal)在这一转型中处于领先地位。”
在这样一个行业里，可能很难区分希望和真正有影响力的技术，但桌面金属公司自成立以来的五年里肯定展示了一些令人印象深刻的突破。除了Lux Capital、NEA、凯鹏华盈(Kleiner Perkins)、福特汽车公司(Ford Motor Company)、谷歌风险投资公司(Google Ventures)和科赫颠覆技术公司(Koch Disruptive Technologies)等知名投资者外，该公司还达成了一些大牌合作伙伴关系。福特和宝马都从公司颠覆汽车制造的潜力中看到了足够的希望，可以签约成为投资者。
note_en:
Desktop Metal’s had  no shortage of investor interest in its business during in its half-decade existence. To date, the metal 3D printing company has raised $430 million, becoming one of the fastest U.S. companies to achieve unicorn status in the process.
 Today it also became the latest in a  recent string of firms to announce its  intentions to go public.
 The Burlington, Massachusetts company plans to list on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol “DM” by merging with  Trine Acquisition Corp. The black-check company announced its  own initial public offering of $261 million in March of last year.
  Under this new deal, Trine will merge with Desktop  Metal to create a company worth  up to $2.5 billion, by its own estimates. Desktop Metal’s most recent valuation from early last year had the company  at $1.5 billion. The SPAC-led deal could therefore provide an attractive mark-up for Desktop Metal investors.
 The deal follows a recent uptick in  SPAC mergers (amid a flurry of activity generally),  including Virgin Galactic.
  The SPAC-led public debut will generate as much as $575 in “gross proceeds,” according to  a release describing the deal. Trine will contribute $300 million, with another $275 million from “committed common stock PIPE at $10.00 per share.” The term PIPE stands for “ private investment in public equity,” and allows for more money to flow into SPAC-led deals when required.
  In addition to furthering its own R&D efforts,  Desktop Metal   plans to make its own acquisitions, in what it refers to as a “constructive consolidation” of the additive manufacturing/3D printing industry. The deal would position Desktop Metal as a primary player in what it’s deemed “additive manufacturing 2.0” — essentially a second wave in 3D printing innovation that could finally see the technology fulfilling its potential to truly upend manufacturing after   years and decades of hype.
 “We are at a major inflection point in the adoption of additive manufacturing, and Desktop Metal is leading the way in this transformation,” founder and CEO Rick Fulop said in a release.
 It can be difficult to distinguish the hope from the truly impactful technologies in an industry like this, but Desktop Metal has certainly demonstrated some impressive breakthroughs in the five years since its founding. In addition to high profile investors like Lux Capital, NEA, Kleiner Perkins, Ford Motor Company, Google Ventures and Koch Disruptive Technologies, the company has struck a number of big name partnerships. Ford and BMW have both seen enough promise in the company’s potential to disrupt auto-making to sign on as investors.
title: Desktop Metal在SPAC牵头的交易中上市，3D打印机公司的估值可能达到25亿美元
title_en: Desktop Metal going public in SPAC-led deal that could value 3D printer company at $2.5B
